name: ena-database
description: Access European Nucleotide Archive via API/FTP. Retrieve DNA/RNA sequences, raw reads (FASTQ), genome assemblies by accession, for genomics and bioinformatics pipelines. Supports multiple formats.
capabilities: Search nucleotide sequences by accession number
Query metadata for studies, samples, and runs
Download genome assemblies in FASTA format
Retrieve raw sequencing reads (FASTQ) and annotations
Access taxonomic information via ENA Taxonomy API
Find cross-references to related database records
author: davila7
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files. No executable code, scripts, or network calls. All static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code delimiters not shell execution; SAM references are BioSample accessions not Windows security accounts; MD5 is documented as file checksum not for cryptographic security; FTP/HTTP patterns are legitimate ENA data retrieval documentation.
valueStatement: Finding and downloading nucleotide sequences requires understanding complex API syntax and multiple endpoint patterns. This skill provides comprehensive documentation for querying the European Nucleotide Archive to retrieve DNA/RNA sequences, genome assemblies, and raw sequencing reads by accession.
limitations: Rate limited to 50 requests per second from ENA APIs
Large file downloads require FTP or Aspera (not direct API)
Does not provide local file caching or pipeline integration
No built-in authentication for restricted datasets

useCases.title: Download reference genomes
useCases.description: Retrieve genome assemblies for a specific organism to use as reference in downstream analysis pipelines

useCases.title: Access raw sequencing data
useCases.description: Find and download FASTQ files from public sequencing projects for comparative genomics studies

useCases.title: Build metadata queries
useCases.description: Search ENA by taxonomy, study accession, or date range to build large-scale dataset collections

promptTemplates.title: Simple accession lookup
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I retrieve the FASTA sequence for accession {accession} from ENA?

promptTemplates.title: Study sample search
promptTemplates.prompt: List all samples associated with study {study_id} using the ENA Portal API

promptTemplates.title: Organism assembly query
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all genome assemblies available for {organism} with assembly level and contig N50 values

promptTemplates.title: Bulk download setup
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the FTP download URLs for all FASTQ files in run {run_accession} and what retry logic should I implement?

bestPractices: Implement exponential backoff to handle HTTP 429 rate limit responses from ENA APIs
Use the filereport endpoint to get FTP URLs for large FASTQ downloads instead of iterating API calls
Cache taxonomy lookups locally when processing many records to reduce API calls
antiPatterns: Making individual API calls for each file in a large batch instead of using FTP bulk downloads
Using XML format when only metadata is needed (prefer JSON or TSV for efficiency)
Ignoring HTTP status codes and failing to handle 404 or 429 responses gracefully

faq.question: Is ENA free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, ENA is a free public repository. No authentication is required for accessing publicly available data.

faq.question: What formats can I download sequences in?
faq.answer: Sequences available in FASTA (nucleotide), XML (metadata), and EMBL flat file format. Raw reads in FASTQ.

faq.question: How do I download large datasets efficiently?
faq.answer: Use the filereport endpoint to get FTP URLs, then download via FTP or Aspera for large files over 100MB.

faq.question: Can I search ENA by organism name?
faq.answer: Yes, use the taxonomy API with tax_tree(taxon_id) or tax_eq() operators in the Portal API query syntax.

faq.question: What happens if I exceed the rate limit?
faq.answer: ENA returns HTTP 429. Implement retry logic with exponential backoff (start with 1 second delay).

faq.question: How is ENA different from NCBI GenBank?
faq.answer: ENA is the European counterpart that exchanges data with GenBank and SRA. Both contain similar datasets with synchronized updates.
